narrative: Dear Maria, imagine the admissions team looking at your application not just as a collection of numbers, but as a story of your journey toward becoming a lawyer, and their model sifted through it to predict your chances on the bar exam. It saw your Year 3 decile of 1.0 as the biggest signal, whispering that even after time to grow, your grades placed you at the bottom of your class, pulling strongly toward the risk of not passing. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 26, which stood out as notably low compared to what typically succeeds, adding real weight to that concern. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 echoed this early pattern, suggesting consistency in academic challenges from the start. With your middle family income, the model noted fewer resources that often buffer struggles like these, tilting things further toward failure. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.3, while solid, didn't quite counterbalance the rest in this picture. Being Hispanic and female, along with planning full-time studies, wove in subtle demographic threads that, in the model's view, aligned with higher hurdles for bar success. Together, these painted a portrait where the odds tipped to a 54.9% chance of not passing, leading to the tough call to reject your application. It's not a judgment on your potential or passion—it's just how these pieces fit the patterns the model knows. You have strengths here, like that UGPA, that could shine brighter with targeted steps forward. Keep pushing; stories like yours often rewrite themselves.
